lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text:
neighbor_story = 0 - havent talked to, 1 - first greeting finished, 
	2 - asked for medicine, 3 - medicine given by doctor, 4 - medicine given to neighbor

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: neighbor_visit_story = 0 - havent visited, 1 - visited neighbors first time

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: neighbor_opinion = 0 - doesnt like you particularly, 1 - likes you somewhat, 2 - real fan

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: Once Again you see the man sitting on the porch this time with a smile on his face.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Ready to meet your neighbor?

choices.text: hello. Sure...?
Leave me alone.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Did you catch that?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Catch what?

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Me asking if youre ready to meet your neighbor.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Im not deaf.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Its a funny play on the saying: Ready to meet your maker.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: I also caught that.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Winston Churchills epitaph is: Im ready to meet my maker but Im not sure hes ready to meet me.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: I could have the same written on MY tombstone with how rude you were last time. With you know-

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: the word maker replaced with neighbor...

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: You know your way around words Ill give you that and yeah sorry about last time. I had a bad morning.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Its okay. Why arent you at school anyway?

choices.text: Ummm. I have a cold? (Youre lying)
Thats confidential. *you wink at him*

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: As you walk near the house a man sitting on the porch looks at you. he looks old and wears camo trousers and a white tank top. You aproach cautiously.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Hey. how is it going young man?

choices.text: hello. Fine...who are you?
Leave me alone.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text:
Im your new neighbor! Me and my family just moved in last week. 
	Figured id get to know the people around here.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Well I'm Jessie. Nice to meet you.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Im Michael. I have a son around your age maybe you could get to know eachother sometime.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: I guess we could.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Hes at school right now. Why arent you as well by the way?

choices.text: Ummm. I have a cold? (Youre lying)
Thats confidential. *you wink at him*

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Ummm. I have a cold? (Youre lying)

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Oh okay. Hope you get better soon then. Bye.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Oh haha. Well try not to get caught then. Adam already got into problems for missing one lesson because of his...

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: *the man shuffles slightly*

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: well..us moving in. Obviously.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Oh that sucks. But dont worry about me. Besides I have some important stuff to do.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Sure. Come by some time in the evening. Adam will be home by then and you can meet.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Sounds good! See you later.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: The neighbor slowly shuffles to the door, holding his lower back while doing so.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Leave me alone.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Wow. No need to be rude...

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Hey kid. Hows it going today?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Cant complain. You seem in a good mood.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: No reason to be sad today so Im smiling.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Seems like a good way of thinking about life.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Heh well..

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Listen, are you free today?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Sure, why?

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: I need my medicine from the hospital, but my back is really hurting so I cant go myself.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: If you could go take it for me Id be really greatful.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Sure, I can go.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Thank you. Id send my boy but hes already left for school. Just take this prescription here and the lady at the hospital will give you the meds.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Okay dont worry Ill go take them.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Hey son.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: What do you need?

choices.text: talk about the medicine
Could you lend me some money?
Ask about their son

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: I got the medicine you asked for.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Oh thank you! You cant even understand how much I owe you.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Where did you say I should go get the medicine from?

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Oh..from the hospital.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: The hospital?

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Yeah. Anything wrong?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: No, no. Just that it must be some special medicine that you have to go to the hospital for it.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Well. Dont bother about what it is. Id really appreciate it if you just went to get it for me. My back is really hurting right now.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Sure, dont worry about it.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: So. How is your son? You said we could meet?

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Oh youre still interested?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Sure. The more connections the better. Haha.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Well come around one of these evenings and you can meet. Hes home almost every day.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Can I ask you something?

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Is the medicine for your son?

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Kid..

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: I like you but youre threading on thin ice here.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: The answer is that it is none of your business. I asked you for a favour not a to be nosey.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Im not trying to be nosey...

choices.text: I was just curious I wont push anymore. Sorry if I offended you.
Im sorry and I wont judge but I think I deserve an answer.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Youre damn right you wont.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: I know youre just a kid but you really should think before you ask rude questions.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: If youre not careful youll get punched in the face a lot when you grow up.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: You think you deserve-

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Listen here you little shit. Either go get the medicine or leave. You dont 'deserve' anything.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: I dont need people gossiping about my son.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: And MOST OF ALL I dont need nosey half-whits spreading lies about my family.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: I would never do such thing. And more importantly-

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Sure. We will not speak about this anymore.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: I gave you the money so go get my medicine.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: You think you deserve-

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Listen here you little shit. Either go get the medicine or leave. You dont 'deserve' anything.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: I dont need people gossiping about my son.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: And MOST OF ALL I dont need nosey half-whits spreading lies about my family.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: I would never do such thing. And more importantly-

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Sure. We will not speak about this anymore.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Either go get my medicine or dont.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Ummm. Could you lend me some money?

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: What for?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Well the medicine. Turns out its not free. Actually its 11 dollars.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Are they for real? Every year they are just milking us more and more.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Soon we wont even have any money to live.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: It is what it is. I dont have any money to spare so...

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Ugh. Im sorry for rambling. Sure, heres the money. And thank you for doing this.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Sure no problem

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: That went better thank expected.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Ummm. Could you lend me some money?

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: What do you mean? I already gave you money for the medicine.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Oh yeah I forgot. Sorry..

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: Looks like the neighbors arent welcoming any visitors at this time. Or maybe its just you...

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Well...If i want to meet that kid I should get on better terms with his father.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Meeting him in school is a no no. Hes too weird to risk being seen with him.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: I should catch up with Michael - the weid military style neighbor. He definitely has a MAGA hat hidden somewhere.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You knock on the door.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You hear somebody shuffling to the door.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Hey kid. You here to see Adam right?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Yeah. I told you id stop by. So Im here.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Hold on hes right upstairs. Let me hollar at im.

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Adaaaaam!!

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: The sound of his voice is so loud youd swear you just got a tinnitus from it

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: Nonetheless nobody comes...

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Maybe hes sleeping?

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: No hes must be doing something important. Wait here for a bit, would ya?

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: The man turns around to walk away and you see a pistol behind his belt

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: *just what I need*

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: Oh hello sweetie.*

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Oh, good evening. Your Adams mom I presume?

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: Step mom actually. But yes, youre correct. Ive raised him from 2 years old so basically you can call me his mom.

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: But sometimes I feel like his doorman, waitress and detail crew with how much I do around here.

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: Not to say he isnt a good boy. Hes the best boy. Thats why hes so good.

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: ...And Michael as well!

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: an awkward silence fills the room

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: Did my husband...

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: ...well..say something about me?

choices.text: Not really.
He mentioned how much you take care of your son. (lie)
He actaully mentioned some medicine that he needed for me to pick up. He couldnt do it himself at the time.

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: He is a man of few words right?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: sur-

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: He really is.

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: Adam is gonna be just as stoic and caring as he is.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You hear walking from upstair

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: IN A GOOD WAY. Stoic as in logical and deliberate!

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: Did he?

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: Like...in a what way?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Like praise kind of way. That hes lucky to have you.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: The lady seems to have noticed the weird posture she has been maintaining for the whole conversation and relaxes a little.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: You hear walking from upstair

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: What? What medicine?

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: Oh I know! Its his back. Hes been working so hard to get all the furniture in order. Poor man.

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: We just moved into the city and I told him not to but he had to and he said he must.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Must what?

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: Move the furniture in!

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: Why didnt he tell me I know just the person for that...

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: Its the Marry girl in town. Her massages are to die for. Have you tried them?

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: Not really. I dont-

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: You definitely should. Last time I went there I felt like I was reborn.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: The lady seems to have noticed the weird posture she has been maintaining for the whole conversation and relaxes a little.

lines.speaker: jessie
lines.text: So its for his back? The packaging didnt seem like just some pain meds. And neither was the place I picked them up from.

lines.speaker: neutral
lines.text: She slowly return to her original posture as she speaks..

lines.speaker: janette
lines.text: Oh they definitely are. Its the doctors, they make it so hard to stay healthy in this day and age. Just last week I-

lines.text: You hear walking from upstair

lines.speaker: neighbor
lines.text: Stop talking his ear off Jane. I told you to keep it down when theres people around.

lines.speaker: david

lines.speaker: david
lines.text: Its the so called state of the art medicine for histerical woman.

lines.speaker: david
lines.text: But its anything but medicine.

lines.speaker: david
lines.text: It makes my mom all...

lines.text: Well youve seen how she is.

lines.speaker: david
